body.title: [open_questions] Shim as persistent front-door on the Ground Loop (daemon + MCP receive) — resolve interface, then decompose
body.intention: I intend that a worker's shim runs persistently on the Ground Loop as a wake-capable front-door, so the heavy device can be dormant while still reachable — verified when Granny's MCP wake-call to a DOWN worker is answered and wakes it.
body.description:
**DESIGN-FIRST (status open_questions):** the crux of D-shim-frontdoor-on-groundloop. The shim moves from living INSIDE the device process to being ATTACHED to the Ground Loop (the existing persistent supervisor, [[project_ground_loop_web_server]]) for daemon lifetime + MCP receive (via skeleton, the MCP aggregator). This makes the shim a wake-capable front-door that outlives its dormant device. This ticket resolves the interface, then the downstream build tickets get filed.

**OPEN QUESTIONS to answer before build (Q&A in-ticket, per open_questions workflow):**
- Q1: one Ground Loop per HOST hosting all worker shims (assumed), or a Ground Loop per shim?
- Q2: the shim<->Ground-Loop attach interface — how does a shim register for daemon lifetime + an MCP receive endpoint through skeleton? (What does BaseShim gain? Does the Ground Loop own a registry of attached shims?)
- Q3: the wake protocol — MCP verb + payload, the 'i'm on it' ack, the spawn/wake mechanics (front-door starts the device process), and the sleep/teardown trigger (idle timeout vs explicit).

**What to build (this ticket):** the attach mechanism — a shim can attach to the Ground Loop and stay up as a front-door with an MCP receive endpoint, independent of its device's liveness; the availability flag becomes 'wakeable' (front-door up) rather than 'device running'. Prove: with the device DOWN, an MCP call to the front-door is received and the front-door can spawn/wake the device.

**Completion criteria:** DickSimnel's shim attaches to the Ground Loop, survives the device being down, receives an MCP wake-call, and wakes the device — demonstrated end to end.

**Test plan (real red->green):** integration — start the Ground Loop with DS's front-door attached, device NOT running; send the MCP wake verb; assert the front-door acks and the device process comes up and receives the ticket. Red before the attach/wake path exists; green after. (Interface Qs must be resolved first — this is why status is open_questions.)

**Validation (runtime):** a wake-call to a down DS.0 produces a front-door ack log ('i'm on it') on the Ground Loop and a subsequent device-start log; the availability flag reads 'wakeable' while the device is down. Observable via Ground Loop logs + `~/.unseen_university/ground_loop/`.

**DOWNSTREAM TICKETS (file AFTER Q1-Q3 resolve — deliberately NOT filed yet, to avoid hollow build tickets on an undesigned interface):** T-shim-lease-instance-numbers (M, wires D-worker-instance-identity leasing), T-granny-wake-not-launch (M, remove granny.yaml DS.0 launch_cmd + daemon static-worker handling), T-granny-mru-and-stall-alarm (M, MRU + stall->system_alarm+resume; links T-alarm-callsites-groundloop-db-install + T-vetinari-owns-alarm-escalation).

**Rollback:** the attach path is additive (new front-door mode); the current in-process shim keeps working until the wake path is proven, so revert = stop attaching. DS is off until free inference so blast radius is low.

**Logging:** front-door MUST log the wake-call receipt, the ack, and the device-spawn at INFO (interface crossings); log ERROR on a failed wake.

**Observable via:** log.info('frontdoor: wake-call received for <worker>') and log.info('frontdoor: spawning device <worker>') on the Ground Loop.

**Affected files (indicative — settle in design):** `unseen_university/devices/skeleton/` (MCP aggregation), the Ground Loop descriptor/host, `unseen_university/devices/<worker>/shim.py` + `BaseShim`. 

**Scope boundary:** the attach + wake front-door mechanism, proven on DickSimnel. Granny MRU/wake-routing, launch_cmd removal, and number leasing are the enumerated downstream tickets, filed after this closes.

**audit-emphasis:** needs-deep-smell (architectural, HIGH blast radius on the shim/ground-loop boundary).

[error] device.py and shim.py are the design center. Every component inherits from — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] bus/ owns comms:// routing. Nothing outside bus/ speaks to IMAP directly. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] skeleton/ owns the MCP aggregator and flat-file registry. No Postgres dependency. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] devices/ contains one subdirectory per device; each is independently deployable. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] `~/.unseen_university/logs/<device>/<stream>/` is the canonical log hierarchy — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] Every ticket closes proven, or declares itself unproven — no discriminator. A ticket that — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)

intention: I intend that a worker's shim runs persistently on the Ground Loop as a runme.py front-door, so the heavy device can be dormant yet reachable — verified when work delivered to a DOWN worker's front-door is received and wakes the device.
description:
**RESOLVED 2026-07-02 (was open_questions; Q1-Q3 answered — see D-shim-frontdoor-on-groundloop).** Build the ATTACH + WAKE front-door foundation.

**The model (resolved):** the Ground Loop is a DUMB per-host daemon loop that launches ONLY runme.py and checks nothing. The shim ships a runme.py the existing RunmeSupervisor loads/supervises/borkedits — that runme.py loop IS the persistent front-door. TWO LEVELS: GL keeps the front-door's runme.py alive; the front-door wakes/spawns and (later) idle-sleeps the heavy device. The web-proxy pattern generalized: the shim is the proxy, the device is the web server; the proxy wakes the server on an incoming request. NO register()/registry on the Ground Loop.

**What to build (THIS ticket — attach + wake only):**
- The shim attaches to the Ground Loop by shipping a runme.py the RunmeSupervisor runs; the front-door loop stays up independent of the device's liveness.
- The availability flag becomes 'WAKEABLE' (front-door up), not 'device running'; the front-door writes it.
- With the device DOWN, work / an MCP call arriving at the front-door is RECEIVED and the front-door spawns/wakes the device (subprocess, plugin_proxy.Popen style) and hands off the work.

**Out of scope (separate downstream tickets, now filed):** number leasing + data dirs (T-shim-lease-instance-numbers), pool/singleton/wipe semantics (T-worker-pool-semantics), idle-sleep bus-ask (T-worker-idle-sleep-bus-ask), Granny wake-not-launch + launch_cmd removal (T-granny-wake-not-launch), MRU + stall alarm (T-granny-mru-and-stall-alarm).

**Completion criteria:** DickSimnel's shim attaches to the Ground Loop as a runme.py front-door, survives the device being down, receives work, and spawns/wakes the device end to end; the availability flag reads 'wakeable' while the device is dormant.

**Test plan (real red->green):** integration — start the Ground Loop with DS's front-door runme.py attached, device NOT running; deliver work to the front-door; assert the front-door receives it AND the device process comes up and gets the work. Red before the attach/wake path exists; green after.

**Validation (runtime):** a wake to a down DS.0 produces a front-door 'i'm on it'/spawn log on the Ground Loop + a subsequent device-start log; availability reads 'wakeable' while down. Observable via Ground Loop logs + ~/.unseen_university/ground_loop/.

**Rollback:** additive (new front-door mode); the current in-process shim keeps working until the wake path is proven, so revert = stop attaching. DS is off until free inference, blast radius low.

**Logging:** front-door MUST log wake-receipt, the ack, and device-spawn at INFO (interface crossings); ERROR on a failed wake.

**Observable via:** log.info('frontdoor: wake received for <worker>') and log.info('frontdoor: spawning device <worker>') on the Ground Loop.

**Affected files (indicative):** unseen_university/devices/ground_loop/ (RunmeSupervisor already loads runme.py), the shim + a shipped runme.py, unseen_university/devices/<worker>/shim.py + BaseShim.

**audit-emphasis:** needs-deep-smell (architectural, HIGH blast radius on the shim/ground-loop boundary).

**Scope boundary:** the attach + wake front-door mechanism, proven on DickSimnel. Everything else is the enumerated downstream tickets.
